bullet_points: Ideas should be based on the Lagrangian in https://arxiv.org/abs/2012.10218.
Each idea must explicitly link to observational constraints: speed of gravitational waves, cosmological expansion, and structure growth.
For each idea, briefly explain which hidden symmetry might address ghost/tachyon issues in an FRW background.
Consider the impact of symmetries on ghost/tachyon reappearance as discussed in https://arxiv.org/pdf/2004.11655.
Ideas should be suitable for analysis in an FRW background.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Generate 5 new research project ideas related to the analysis of type I DHOST theories with massless gravity, focusing on hidden symmetries and stability (ghost/tachyon avoidance), as well as compatibility with observational constraints.

bullet_points: Evaluate each idea for originality, clarity, and scientific value.
Clearly identify any potential mathematical or physical inconsistencies arising from the use of the Lagrangian.
Provide actionable feedback on how these challenges might be overcome.
Suggest how each idea could be improved or why it should be discarded.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Critique the 5 research project ideas, identifying weaknesses, feasibility issues, and suggesting improvements or eliminations.

bullet_points: Choose the 2 most promising ideas, incorporating feedback from idea_hater.
Refine the ideas to address any weaknesses or gaps.
Explicitly incorporate any necessary transformations or symmetry-building mechanisms that could be feasibly embedded in the theory.
Ensure the improved ideas are well-aligned with the original problem statement.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Select and improve 2 out of the 5 research project ideas based on the critiques.

bullet_points: Provide a detailed critique of each improved idea.
Ensure that the improved ideas maintain consistency with both the theoretical constraints (e.g., ghost absence at one-loop level) and the observational constraints.
Emphasize the strengths in concrete terms.
Suggest any final improvements or considerations.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Critique the 2 improved research project ideas, focusing on their strengths and remaining weaknesses.

bullet_points: Choose the single most promising and feasible research project idea.
Justify the selection based on critiques and alignment with the problem statement.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Select the best idea out of the 2 improved ones.

bullet_points: Create a concise, informative paper title.
Write a 5-sentence abstract/description summarizing the research project’s goals, methods, and significance.
Ensure the title and abstract clearly reflect the project’s novelty, feasibility, and relevance to both gravitational theory and cosmological observations.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Report the best idea in the form of a scientific paper title with a 5-sentence description.
